[Home](./index.md) &gt; [@remobj/core](./core.md)

## core package

## Functions

<table><thead><tr><th>

Function


</th><th>

Description


</th></tr></thead>
<tbody><tr><td>

[connectEndpoints(endpointA, endpointB)](./core.connectendpoints.md)


</td><td>

Establishes bidirectional communication between two PostMessageEndpoints.

All messages sent to endpointA will be forwarded to endpointB and vice versa.

This creates a communication bridge, useful for: - Connecting different communication contexts - Creating proxy/relay patterns - Bridging incompatible endpoint types


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[consume(endpoint)](./core.consume.md)


</td><td>

Creates a proxy object for consuming remote objects over a PostMessageEndpoint.

T - The type of the remote object for complete type safety


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[createChannel(endpoint, channelName)](./core.createchannel.md)


</td><td>

Creates an isolated communication channel over a shared PostMessageEndpoint.

Messages are filtered by channel name, allowing multiple independent communication streams over a single endpoint.

This is useful for: - Creating separate APIs over one Worker connection - Isolating different communication contexts - Implementing multiplexed communication protocols


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[createProxyEndpoint(targetEndpoint, options)](./core.createproxyendpoint.md)


</td><td>

Creates a proxy endpoint that wraps another endpoint for devtools monitoring


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[provide(obj, endpoint)](./core.provide.md)


</td><td>

Exposes an object's methods and properties for remote access over a PostMessageEndpoint.

T - The type of the object being provided for type inference on consumer side


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[setMonitorEndpoint(endpoint)](./core.setmonitorendpoint.md)


</td><td>

Sets the monitor endpoint for devtools communication


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[withLogging(endpoint, options)](./core.withlogging.md)


</td><td>

Wraps a PostMessageEndpoint with logging capabilities for debugging and monitoring.

Logs incoming and/or outgoing messages with configurable options.

This is useful for: - Debugging communication issues - Monitoring message flow - Adding custom logging/telemetry


</td></tr>
</tbody></table>

T - The message data type (defaults to any)


</td></tr>
